commands.name: schema
commands.description: Print CLI schema.

commands.name: ask
commands.description: Interpret a small natural-language request such as checking, releasing, listing history, or rolling back a configured service, then execute the matching service-level OpsPilot command. Use --dry-run to only show the interpreted command.

commands.name: inventory overview
commands.description: Return cluster, server, workload, pod, and container overview.

commands.name: metrics health
commands.description: Check whether OpsPilot can reach configured Prometheus datasources.

commands.name: metrics datasources
commands.description: List configured Prometheus datasources, default source, and reachability.

commands.name: metrics query
commands.description: Run a read-only PromQL instant query through OpsPilot Core. Supports --source.

commands.name: metrics nodes
commands.description: Return node CPU, memory, and root filesystem usage from Prometheus. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: metrics pods
commands.description: Return top Pod CPU and memory usage from Prometheus, optionally filtered by namespace. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: metrics containers
commands.description: Return top Docker/container CPU and memory usage from cAdvisor metrics. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: metrics pod
commands.description: Return CPU, memory, and restart metrics for one Pod. Supports --source.

commands.name: metrics filesystems
commands.description: Return filesystem mountpoint free space, total space, and usage percent from Prometheus. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: k8s pods
commands.description: List Kubernetes Pods with optional namespace, status, and query filters.

commands.name: k8s logs pod
commands.description: Read short-window Kubernetes Pod logs through pods/log.

commands.name: docker agents
commands.description: List configured Docker node agents and reachability.

commands.name: docker containers
commands.description: List allowed Docker containers through a node agent. Supports --host and --host all.

commands.name: docker inspect
commands.description: Return read-only Docker inspect summary for one allowed container.

commands.name: docker logs
commands.description: Read short-window Docker container logs through a node agent.

commands.name: docker stats
commands.description: Return one-shot Docker container stats through a node agent.

commands.name: logs search
commands.description: Search collected Kubernetes logs from the configured OpenSearch/Elasticsearch backend. Supports -q and --query.

commands.name: evidence request
commands.description: Correlate APISIX access logs with service logs by host, URI, time window, optional route mapping, and evidence strength. Supports --skip-apisix / --service-only for preliminary investigation without gateway logs.

commands.name: errors recent
commands.description: Return recent AI-readable error events from Kubernetes abnormal Pods, Argo CD application state, release evidence gaps, and structured file events such as middleware provisioning failures.

commands.name: context pod
commands.description: Return a Pod Evidence Pack.

commands.name: diagnose pod
commands.description: Diagnose a Pod using metrics, events, short-window logs, and release context.

commands.name: diagnose docker
commands.description: Diagnose one Docker container using inspect, stats, and short-window logs.

commands.name: inspect service
commands.description: Return a service-level inspection by service name, including release status, matching Pods, restart count, CPU, memory, Kubernetes log presence, ELK log presence, evidence gaps, and next checks.

commands.name: inspect pod
commands.description: Return a compact Pod inspection with status, restarts, events/context, metrics, Kubernetes log presence, ELK log presence, and evidence gaps.

commands.name: inspect cluster
commands.description: Return a compact cluster inspection with abnormal Pods, node resources, top Pod usage, restart evidence, and filesystem mountpoints.

commands.name: release service
commands.description: Return a beginner-friendly release summary for one configured service, including GitLab pipeline, BuildKit, GitOps, Argo CD, latest jobs, history, gaps, and the current trigger boundary.

commands.name: release trigger
commands.description: Submit a GitLab pipeline for one configured release service through OpsPilot. This follows the standard GitLab Runner -> BuildKit -> Registry -> GitOps -> Argo CD path.

commands.name: release status
commands.description: Return a read-only release evidence chain status for one configured service.

commands.name: release evidence
commands.description: Alias for release status in the first read-only release evidence milestone.

commands.name: release diagnose
commands.description: Alias for release status with explicit evidence gaps in the first release milestone.

commands.name: release jobs
commands.description: List GitLab jobs from the latest pipeline for a configured release service.

commands.name: release logs
commands.description: Read bounded GitLab job trace logs by --job or --job-id for a configured release service.

commands.name: release history
commands.description: List GitOps release history for one configured service, including revision, image tag, current marker, author, date, and commit message.

commands.name: release rollback
commands.description: Rollback a configured service by committing a previous tag, full image, or GitOps revision to the GitOps repository. Requires --confirm and does not mutate Kubernetes directly.

commands.name: onboard repo
commands.description: One-command beginner onboarding for a GitLab project path. Detects the repository, generates platform Dockerfile/CI/deploy files with --write, runs preflight when writing, and prints the release mapping and next steps.

commands.name: onboard service
commands.description: Generate missing Dockerfile, GitLab CI include, Kubernetes manifests, and OpsPilot release mapping from opspilot.service.yaml. Existing files are skipped unless --force is used.

commands.name: onboard check
commands.description: Check whether a service repository has Dockerfile, BuildKit GitLab CI, namespace.yaml, deploy/k8s manifests, and release mapping readiness. Supports --repo and --config.

commands.name: onboard detect
commands.description: Detect service onboarding config from a repository, including language, Dockerfile, port, build entry, tpo/devex ownership, project-level namespace mapping, and shared middleware intent.

commands.name: onboard generate
commands.description: Generate opspilot.service.yaml, GitLab CI include, Dockerfile when missing, namespace.yaml, deploy/k8s manifests, release mapping, and shared middleware intent from repository detection and project-level namespace automation.

commands.name: repo preflight
commands.description: Run automated repository governance checks for Dockerfile, platform GitLab CI include, deploy/k8s manifests, namespace ownership, port, health evidence, and shared middleware intent. Intended for CI gating.

commands.name: repo autofix
commands.description: Generate or replace platform-managed Dockerfile, .gitlab-ci.yml include, deploy/k8s manifests, opspilot.service.yaml, and release mapping. Use --write to mutate files and --force to replace existing generated surfaces.
